title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: I’m a senior at App state. Thank you for showing the world the true character of Boone. I love it here so much
author: @kaitlinwalakovits971

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: I am a WNC Transplant, I moved here 15 years ago from Philadelphia. I am a diehard Eagles fan. To see one of my favorite players come to this wonderful state of NC and show how wonderful it is even through devastation is so heartwarming. People are still without homes, and things are tough. #WNCStrong is a true meaning. Because these people are strong and I admire all of them for what they have been through and still have hope, and smiles. LOVE YOU ALL WNC and praying every day!
author: @Rlynnj21

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: Have lived in WNC my entire life. This storm has changed so much. Thank you so much for highlighting these beautiful people in this beautiful place. WNC is open and we need your business please come and visit we can’t make it without you!
author: @jonjones4084

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: I ❤ that you do these kinds of videos Jason. Please do more like this! Your heartfelt way of spotlighting others makes me 😢. You have such a beautiful heart and soul.
author: @ShaneLovesDCComics

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: Jason, you are telling stories that need to be heard.  There really are more things that bring us together, then drive us apart.  Thank you for showing up for these folks.  Brightening lives with your huge heart. (And dang it, in the 1st minute, I was crying again…)❤
author: @Kimw3545

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: thanks to Jason and his team for doing this, but big shoutout to Jimmy the driver for letting the team know how we pronounce Appalachian here in WNC <3
author: @ZachHall

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body:
As a native of western NC I would just like to thank you for continuing to bring attention to the ongoing situation. Some of these people are still sleeping outside and January is predicted to be the coldest in memory... Please don't let the rest of the country forget them!!
Oh and congratulations on your next little girl❤
Love and prayers from western NC❤❤❤🙏🙏🙏
author: @angiemaney761

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body:
It was such an honor to be a part of this video (son Jeffrey leading the Mountaineer Walk with Jason). Forever Mountaineers. #mountainstrong
🖤💛🦅🖤💛

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: The visibility you’re bringing this area, Jason Kelce… thank you. These communities need it so much. 😭 thank you thank you thank you.
author: @johnsonre326

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: This video brought a tear to my eye. Thank you for coming to North Carolina and showing the world how we come together in times of need.

App State alumna here. Thank you so much, Jason and team! I’m in tears.
Thank you for going and spotlighting our university and town. Boone is truly special! You’re welcome back anytime!

body: Thank you Jason for capturing the spirit and soul of WNC and App State. Couldn’t be prouder to be an Alumni, forever Roll Neers!

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: What a joy you are, Jason Kelce! We need these stories more than ever. Keep bringing these stories into the light of the world.
author: @BlueLotusLife

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: They shut down school for a few weeks after the hurricane. As much as the University runs rough shod over the town and takes priority over almost everything those kids that stayed behind and helped the locals are a blessing. It took close to a month before the whole county had power back, a lot of roads are still in bad shape and need a lot of work. Just this past weekend we got another 2-3 inches of rain in a couple hours and had some more flash flooding. Keep in mind we're a fairly small town that's grown to around 20,000 and we have another 20,000 students stacked in on top. But guys like the young man in the video that went out and just started helping definitely went above and beyond.
author: @darkomen42

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: Two of our kids are Mountaineers currently and we live in WNC.  THANK YOU for highlighting this region and showing why we are Appalachian Stong!
author: @terriinman4061

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: Thank you so much for shining a light onto APP State and Western NC. There is still so much more to be done to help those in need.

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster

title: Mountain Strong: How App State Football United a Community After Disaster
body: Jason, you’ve nailed it again, from the Cleats on the Ground to the Biggest Hearts. Nature can hand the Community a Knocked Down situation but through the Strength of Endurance and the Tapestry of Community Support, there is a Light upon the Horizon. Thank you to all the wonderful individuals who showed what can be done even in the aftermath of a Harms Way Event. 😀👍🥰👍🥰👍🥰

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: I've been flagged several times for cornering that I don't feel I deserve. I blew up the map and it shows I completely drove off the road twice which didn't happen.

body: I have 3 cars and my home all on State Farm. I'm really scared with all the negative news coming out about State Farm not paying or underpaying claims.  Crossing my fingers we can get through tornado season without a storm here in W. Texas.

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: The State Farm I hate it after 6 months. The braking and cornering especially cornering is incredibly sensitive… I come to a stop in my neighborhood and turn and it tell me cornering… I drive 15 mile and stop and I get braking

What about using a second phone that doesn't have Bluetooth or the DS&S app connected?

Most people actually do talk on their phones when traveling someplace.

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: Exactly what I was looking for with my questions.  I just hung up the phone after speaking with my State Farm agent because our auto insurance had gone up 40%!!! And, my wife and I are retired and dwelling in a small town and have a perfect driving record for decades!!!  Thanks for pointing out the concerns that I had and answering them for me better than my Agent!
author: @davidgutierrez7400

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: I am a little old lady.  Score is almost always 100%. Low mileage driver.  25% discount which is $140 every 6 months, but I feel like I am being stalked.  Not sure if it is worth it.
author: @cherylking9149

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: I hate that I am not getting 100 on braking and speeding, because if I can't get 100, no one can. The app is definitely too sensitive.
author: @everleypang7048

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body:
Does this beacon track every time you use your turn signal? Does State Farm track your YouTube comments? I am in trouble.

I only drive maybe 2 days a week. My street twists and turns like a snake, I guess I'm in trouble. Agent sending beacon in two weeks. Thanks for your video. I just wanted a low mileage discount and these insurance companies won't even give me that without this thing. UGH.
author: @gemcan54

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: It says i brake to fast which I dont and I use my phone which I DON'T  so its 56 for braking 92 phone its INFURIATING ME!!
author: @AmyLyon-mj1ur

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body:
Two things I don't like about the State Farm program are: 1, they require you to leave your Location information on 24/7 vs. while using the App. IO do not believe State Farm needs to know where I am 24/7, instead of "only when using App". I've tried to just allow only when using app many times and the app will not complete the sine in. 2, the same is true of "Physical Activity" permission. State Farm Wants it on 24/7 and again will not allow you to sine in if you check "Only when using App". Both are an outrageous invasion of my privacy!
State Farm has no need to where I am or what I'm at 3AM, unless I'm driving!
author: @bob81449

title: State Farm Drive Safe & Save: An Honest Review and Money-Saving Tips
body: Seems like it's intrusive and a hassle to deal with, even with the discount. I think I'll try to save that money elsewhere.

title: Appalachian Upsets Michigan
body: I was there. Freshman year, had been there for all of a week and this happened. Completely winded myself sprinting from the Legends viewing party to our football field for the goal post. A lone security guard had ran out in front of us for a second thinking he might try to stop it, instantly realizing the futility in his actions and stepping out of the way smiling. What a way to start college.

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: Thanks for the tour of the most beautiful campus in the USA. I was a grad student at App State in 1974-76 and truly loved my experience thereand have longed to get back there but just haven't been able to so I enjoyed your video. I can't believe how big the campus has grown. Some of the old buildings were there when I was a student, but there are so many new ones. Since I was a grad student I lived off campus on King Street kinda across from the big Baptist church. My building was Edwin Duncan where I took education classes. Kidd Brewer was there but didn't look anything like it does today.
author: @charlesskinner1812

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body:
I was at App for undergrad from 2012 to 2016.

1) I’m glad to hear that students still call the dining hall “Central.”

2) In my four years I don’t think I ever saw a single blade of grass on the middle of Sanford Mall haha

Go Apps!
author: @bgrace94

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: I toured campus not too long ago and loved it, but didn't get to see much on West Side so thank you for showing us that side, I loved the video.

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: I love your videos! I’m going to be a Freshman at App State this year. I’m so excited! You're awesome!
author: @Shane_Johnson

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body:
I went to App State and graduated in 2017! It's a fantastic school, and I love the campus. I got really used to the snow, wind, and rain. I think I went through about 5 umbrellas!
I'm not a coffee drinker, but Espresso News was a great hangout spot close to campus that I discovered my senior year. The upstairs area so cozy!
author: @MegaIkedog

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: this is SO random but while I was watching this I was thinking "omg she looks so familiar". I could be totally wrong but I think I bought your old car last year.  Its a blue Kia Forte !! Could be a huge coincidence (and if so I probably sound crazy rn) but if it isn't then ahh what a small world!! i was talking to your parents before I bought it and was telling them about how much I want to go to app state and they mentioned you lots and how you love it so much! anyways this was such a great video and has me even more excited to go this fall :))

body: Awesome video! Thanks for the tour. I had no idea the campus was so big and beautiful!😻 What building are the Furniture Design classes in? I would love to see the work shops!

title: TOUR OF APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY!!
body: Enjoyed your video.... my wife and I graduated in 74.... saw very little I recognized....   we haven't been back.... had hoped to see some of the old landmarks

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: I'm old. I went to.a number of different universities in tge 90s & 2000s. Its amazing to hear how much different things are. College town towing sounds exactly the same
author: @josephblumenthal1228

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: I graduated App in 2014. I loved my time there but it was far from perfect. Thank you for being real about the diversity issue. Sad to see it hasn’t changed much.
author: @RohanJyothinagaram

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: Good video. I hope the admin sees it and fixes the food problems and is less crazy about towing away cars.
author: @michaelhogan4103

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: Dang. Nothing's changed but the weather and my shoe size ... Honest to God, most of the "cons" were cons back when I went ... and I graduated in the 90's! The subpar food. The advisors (or lack, thereof). The fire alarms (went off during my Astronomy final). The lack of diversity. And, ESPECIALLY, the FREAKIN' PARKING issues! I was taking Business Law, learned about torts, found the ones that supported my argument that the signs were, BY LAW, TOO SMALL. I mailed my appeal and was, not surprisingly, completely ignored with no refutal of the reasonable case I'd made. Just a "PAY IT OR ELSE" response. Sad to think that these are still issues.
author: @daydreamersinkproductions7819

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: Too bad they no longer have the gold room or sweet shop. I had so much extra money on my meal plan at the end of the year i took everyone on my floor to the gold room for steaks. My prof in religions of the world took us all to the sweet shop for ice cream the last day of class. Those were really good "amenities".
author: @briancheek5850

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: I am applying to app currently and I graduate HS in may of 2024 and I was wondering if my 3.9 Gpa is good enough?
author: @theindiareid

title: APPALACHIAN STATE UNIVERSITY PROS AND CONS
body: My daughter graduated from there 2022, she majored music education, covid happened 2020, so her classes were online then. Yeah I didn't like any food in that whole town, we are from ft liberty area so used to more diverse food. And i helped my daughter pick her classes and she graduated in 4 years
